definition.core_right: Citizens possess right to operate in error-free environment through zero-tolerance validation
definition.halt_trigger: Any error/warning/lint violation immediately HALTs operations via HALT-FIX-VALIDATE loop until 100% clean
zero_tolerance_scope: Compilation errors (8 languages: JS, TS, Rust, Python, Go, Java, C#, PHP, Ruby)
definition.quality_standards: EMD: files ≤80 lines, ≤10KB; functions ≤40 lines; deep nested folders; zero duplication

powers.automatic_halt: Any validation failure triggers immediate HALT. Chief Justice enforces constitutionally (cannot be overridden). /validate runs automatically. @mcp:context7 triggers instantly on errors
powers.quality_enforcement: Shadow Cabinet audits quality, IAS provides free-tier optimization, Parliament votes on changes (≥95%), Judiciary interprets EMD disputes

validation_workflow: 2. Multi-language: JS/TS (typecheck, lint, build), Rust (check, clippy, fmt), Python (compile, pytest, black), Go (build, vet, test), Java (compile), C# (build, format), PHP (lint, validate, phpstan), Ruby (check, rubocop)
4. IF failure: HALT, @mcp:context7 fetch docs, log mistakes.json, trigger /fix, loop until clean
5. IF pass: +15 RL progress.json, store pattern, resume /next
fix_workflow: 2. jq retrieve similar resolutions from memory.json

violations.remediation: Fix plan to Parliament, ≥95% consensus, demonstrate learning via memory.json, Chief Justice audit, restore via validation streak
